notes_for_consumers: Feeds environment prompts and footage queries. Pick settings, objects, and signifiers from the resolved block; lane content is additive on the baseline. Settings-and-objects only; sensory mood lives in creative_intent. The 'no people in frame' rule is honored absolutely — multigenerational and gathering evidence is rendered through objects (multiple coolers, multiple chairs, table set up), never bodies. The audience here is Proud Workhorses, the current core.

content.lane_note: Similar income range to Great Lakes (8th-9th decile in Phoenix metro and Hill Country, more variable in Rio Grande Valley). Multigenerational household composition is high; Mexican-heritage population is the founding population in much of the BC. Maker and outdoor culture present but reorganized around heat: early-morning timing, carport-not-garage, summer as the season that defines life rhythm.

settings.cue: Carport (not garage), open-air, often metal-roofed, shading the truck at midday
Adobe or stucco home, single-story, earth-toned
Open desert two-lane (mesquite, creosote, saguaro depending on sub-region)
Hill Country ranch with live oak and cedar
Border-town main street with bilingual signage
Lake recreation (Travis, Powell, Mead) with boat in tow, often pre-dawn departure
Ranch property with livestock, horse trailer setup
High school football stadium and parking lot in fall
Construction site, often early-morning to avoid heat
Tractor Supply, feed store, farm-supply parking

objects.cue: Bilingual signage in the frame (storefront, road sign, billboard)
Coolers staged for pre-dawn lake or hunting departure
Hunting gear specific to regional game: white-tail (TX), elk (NM), dove (AZ in September)
ATV / UTV with desert configuration (sand tires, brush guards)
Truck mounted with ranch or construction gear: gooseneck hitch, headache rack, toolbox, ladder rack
Mountain bikes, kayaks (Hill Country, AZ recreation culture)
Trailer with grilling and cooking setup for tailgate or family gathering

signifiers.cue: Shade pattern across the truck from a carport, palo verde, or live oak
Tow scenario in pre-dawn or post-sunset light
Truck dust-covered from a ranch or desert road
Multigenerational gathering's evidence around the truck or carport: multiple coolers, multiple chairs, table set up (no people in frame)
Mountain ridgeline visible at distance from most metros
